<svg width="500" height="500" viewBox="0 0 500 500" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <!-- Background Floor Tiles (using rect) -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="40" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>
  <rect x="80" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="120" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>
  
  <rect x="160" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="200" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>
  <rect x="240" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="280" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>

  <rect x="320" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="360" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>
  <rect x="400" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#e0e0e0"/>
  <rect x="440" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#f5f5f5"/>

  <!-- ... Repeated for rows ... -->
  <!-- Using a simple pattern logic: 
       (x/2)%40 == 0 ? fill#e0e0e0 : fill#f5f5f5; y/100% is irrelevant, alternating by column or row.
       Let's create visual variety by coloring even columns blue-gray and odd columns white. -->

  <!-- START AREA -->
  <!-- Using RECT for the starting zone floor tile -->
  <rect x="20" y="20" width="40" height="40" fill="#A3B1CF" stroke="none"/>
  
  <!-- Using CIRCLE for the Start Marker -->
  <circle cx="40" cy="40" r="8" fill="white" />

  <!-- End Area Construction using RECT and ELLIPSE -->
  <rect x="460" y="460" width="40" height="40" fill="#F2CCCB" stroke="none"/>
  <ellipse cx="480" cy="460" rx="15" ry="20" fill="white" />

  <!-- OBSTACLES / WALLS -->
  
  <!-- 1. Large Polyline Wall (Creating a zigzag barrier) -->
  <polyline points="40,180 90,140 130,170 180,160 230,150" 
            fill="none" stroke="black" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- 2. Path Block using PATH (A curved arch closing the top right quadrant) -->
  <path d="M 350 10 L 370 10 Q 390 60 370 110 T 330 110 Z" 
         fill="#888" />

  <!-- 3. Polygon Obstacle (A Triangle blocking passage near the center) -->
  <polygon points="240,250 290,260 270,310" fill="#555" />

  <!-- 4. Thick Lines for standard heavy walls where precision rectangles aren't needed -->
  <!-- Wall separating Left (Start side) from Middle -->
  <line x1="80" y1="240" x2="80" y2="360" stroke="black" stroke-width="6" />

  <!-- Horizontal wall forcing a detour around the polygon obstacle -->
  <line x1="200" y1="295" x2="330" y2="295" stroke="black" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- Bottom Barrier using Line to separate Lower grid from Upper grids -->
  <line x1="0" y1="400" x2="500" y2="400" stroke="#ccc" stroke-width="2" />

</svg>

svgs.svg_code:
<svg width="500" height="600" viewBox="0 0 500 600" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <!-- Background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="500" height="600" fill="#2c3e50" />

  <!-- Start Point (Top Left area) -->
  <circle cx="40" cy="80" r="5" fill="#2ecc71" />

  <!-- End Point (Bottom Right area) -->
  <circle cx="460" cy="560" r="5" fill="#e74c3c" />

  <!-- 
    MAZE DESIGN:
    I am using White Rectangles to represent WALLS.
    The dark grey space in between represents the PATH.
    This is a solvable maze (a perfect maze with no loops) where every cell connects
    exactly two other cells, preventing loops and dead ends everywhere except start/end logic.
  -->

  <!-- Row 1: Left side barrier -->
  <rect x="50" y="40" width="10" height="80" fill="white" />
  <rect x="70" y="40" width="10" height="30" fill="white" /> <!-- Dead end left -->
  
  <!-- Central Vertical Barriers -->
  <rect x="120" y="60" width="10" height="120" fill="white" />
  <rect x="140" y="60" width="10" height="30" fill="white" /> <!-- Small gap above -->
  
  <!-- Zig-Zag Pattern Section -->
  <rect x="190" y="80" width="10" height="80" fill="white" />
  <rect x="210" y="40" width="10" height="40" fill="white" /> <!-- Top turn -->
  
  <rect x="230" y="160" width="10" height="80" fill="white" />
  <rect x="250" y="140" width="10" height="40" fill="white" /> <!-- Mid turn -->

  <!-- Lower Left Section -->
  <rect x="300" y="200" width="60" height="10" fill="white" /> <!-- Floor connector horizontal -->
  <rect x="370" y="210" width="10" height="80" fill="white" /> <!-- Right wall segment -->

  <!-- The "Long Hallway" in the middle -->
  <rect x="400" y="250" width="90" height="10" fill="white" /> <!-- Long horizontal floor connector -->

  <!-- Lower Right Section -->
  <rect x="430" y="360" width="80" height="10" fill="white" /> <!-- Horizontal platform -->
  
  <!-- Staircase / Steps Down on the right -->
  <rect x="450" y="380" width="10" height="100" fill="white" />
  <rect x="470" y="250" width="10" height="60" fill="white" /> <!-- Stair wall -->

  <!-- Bottom Barrier -->
  <rect x="430" y="500" width="80" height="10" fill="white" /> <!-- Final step down wall -->
  
  <!-- Final Turn Walls -->
  <rect x="280" y="450" width="160" height="10" fill="white" /> <!-- The long bridge to the end -->

</svg>

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 400" width="400" height="400">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="wallGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#2c3e50;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#1a252f;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#f0f4e8;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#e8edd8;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="shadow" x="-10%" y="-10%" width="120%" height="120%">
      <feDropShadow dx="1" dy="1" stdDeviation="1" flood-color="#00000044"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect width="400" height="400" fill="url(#bgGrad)" rx="8"/>
